# VER-QUIC-CRT-0019 - QUIC CRT client-role 1-RTT readiness verification

## Scope

Verify that client-role Finished processing publishes the first 1-RTT packet-protection material only after peer Finished verification and that the supported active-loopback stream boundary remains reachable without widening transfer or retry support.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0121

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and benchmark evidence.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic library builds successfully on net10.0.
- The benchmark project restores successfully.
- The supported client loopback path can be exercised locally.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the new client-role 1-RTT readiness requirement-home test.
- Run the affected CRT requirement-home tests that exercise the client Finished path and the bridge gate.
- Run the supported interop/API guards that cover the loopback stream-open boundary.
- Run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
- Run the permanent benchmark suite for the client Finished publication path.
- Re-run the repo-local SpecTrace validation checks and markdown rendering checks after the artifact updates.

## Expected Result

- The client Finished path publishes `PeerFinishedVerified`, `KeysAvailable(OneRtt)`, and directional 1-RTT packet-protection material in the supported order.
- Premature `OneRtt` key publication is rejected before `PeerFinishedVerified`.
- The supported loopback stream-open and stream-capacity guards remain green after the readiness seam lands.
- The permanent client Finished benchmark exists and can be run locally.
- Transfer and retry remain unsupported.
